surface_verification.justification: cc3/AR-05 is a compliance-reporting/library change: rtm_generator gains a per-FR Reconciled? column + an 'FRs needing re-verification' summary subsection driven by the shared BP-2 _reconciliation helper, plus readability fixes (full FR titles, clickable evt- evidence, column legend, Last Verified->Last tested) and removal of the age-based stale clause; cohesive helpers extracted to a new leaf module for anti-ratchet. No product web/api/cli/UI/store surface changes; the RTM is a static tracked .md. Verified empirically against the real event log: the RTM Reconciled? column (4 check, 0 warn, 10 dash) is byte-identical to the Control-Grade reconciliation dimension (4 behavior-touched, 0 unreconciled) - both read the one helper. Full suites green: compliance 780, shared 3596, iterate 436, integration 174; pinned-ruff clean; anti-ratchet 0 ratchets.

behaviors.behavior: Reconciled? column renders the helper's per-FR status (check / needs-re-verification / dash)
Reconciled? fires only on touched-without-re-verify, never on age
RTM Reconciled? column agrees with the Control-Grade reconciliation dimension (one shared helper)
behaviors.evidence: TestColumnAgreesWithHelper::test_per_fr_status_matches_compute_reconciliation PASSED + real-event-log probe (RTM 4 check/0 warn/10 dash == grade 4 touched/0 unreconciled)

behaviors.behavior: Age-based '>14d stale verification' clause removed; reconciliation-driven 'FRs needing re-verification' subsection added
'Last Verified' column renamed to neutral 'Last tested'
evt- ids clickable, linked to a Verification Timeline anchor; malformed ids stay plain text
Full FR titles (no 60-char truncation) in the coverage table
Column legend decodes Tests / Last tested / Reconciled?
Reconciled? glyph map keys can't drift from the helper's status constants
Minimal-env fallback: helper unimportable degrades the column to '—', never crashes the render
behaviors.evidence: TestNullReconciliationFallback (null reads untouched; safe wrapper returns usable object) PASSED
